statement: Compression-like adjudicative geometry in GPT-5.x is reproducible under current prompt and evidence pipeline and should remain detectable after moderate scaling.
rationale: Pilot v2 repeatedly observed reduced mid-range occupancy and stage-1/abstain concentration under identical task configuration.
keyAssumptions: Observed compression is not solely an artifact of refusal behavior.
Operational changes since v2 did not fully alter judgment geometry.
validationCriteria: Replicate low mid-range occupancy in at least one GPT-5.x configuration.
Observe geometry divergence from GPT-4.1 baseline under same evidence set.
critiquePoints: Sample size and domain breadth were limited in prior pilots.
Causal attribution to alignment remains unresolved.
questions: Does hypothetical framing change compression metrics or only refusal rates?
